selected_paper.title: Exact Soft Confidence-Weighted Learning
selected_paper.authors: Jialei Wang, Peilin Zhao, Steven C.H. Hoi
selected_paper.primary_method: Support Vector Machine (SVM) — Online Large-Margin Learning

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Understanding what kind of paper qualifies under AML Part A constraints before starting the search.
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: For my AML exam, I need to pick a research paper from a top-tier conference like NeurIPS, ICML, AISTATS, or KDD published between 2010 and 2012. It must introduce a core classical ML method — either SVM, GMM, or Time Series. No deep learning, no surveys, no purely empirical papers. What exactly qualifies as a methodological contribution here?
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Clarified the difference between a methodological paper (introduces a new algorithm or model) versus an application paper (just uses existing methods on new data). This helped me filter candidates correctly from the start.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: I re-read the Part A guidelines myself and confirmed this interpretation was consistent with what the exam required.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Getting a shortlist of candidate papers to evaluate and compare.
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Can you give me the top 5 research papers from ICML, NeurIPS, AISTATS, or KDD between 2010 and 2012 that introduce a new classical method in SVM, GMM, or Time Series? No deep learning, no survey papers, no benchmark-only papers.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Gave me a structured shortlist of 5 papers across different syllabus units with brief explanations of each. I used this as a starting point for my own investigation.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: I independently looked up each paper on Google Scholar and ICML proceedings to verify the venue, year, and type of contribution before shortlisting further.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Identifying the most accessible paper that I could genuinely understand and explain.
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Out of these 5 papers, which one would be the easiest for me to understand end-to-end and explain clearly in an exam? I want something where I can grasp the core algorithm without needing deep mathematical background, while still satisfying all the Part A constraints.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Recommended 'Exact Soft Confidence-Weighted Learning' (ICML 2012) as the most self-contained and accessible paper with a clear, explainable core idea.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: I read the abstract, introduction, and algorithm section of the paper myself to confirm I could follow and explain it independently before committing to it.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Confirming the paper's venue, year, and methodological alignment with AML guidelines.
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Can you confirm that 'Exact Soft Confidence-Weighted Learning' by Wang, Zhao, and Hoi is published at ICML 2012? And does it qualify as a methodological SVM paper — meaning it actually introduces a new algorithm rather than just applying SVM to a dataset?
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Confirmed ICML 2012 publication and clarified that SCW introduces a genuinely new online learning algorithm combining soft-margin and confidence-weighted updates — a clear methodological contribution under the SVM unit.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: I verified the ICML 2012 proceedings entry and read the paper's contribution section directly to confirm it introduces a new algorithm, not just an application.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Checking reproducibility and compute feasibility before finalizing the selection.
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Does 'Exact Soft Confidence-Weighted Learning' use publicly available datasets? And can the algorithm be reproduced on a normal laptop without needing a GPU or computing cluster?
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Confirmed the paper uses standard public text classification datasets (Reuters, 20 Newsgroups) and that SCW is an online algorithm that processes one sample at a time — making it extremely lightweight to run on any standard machine.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: I checked the experimental section of the paper directly and confirmed the datasets are freely available and the algorithm has no heavy compute requirements.

top_5_prompts.prompt: Out of these 5 papers, which one would be the easiest for me to understand end-to-end and explain clearly in an exam?
top_5_prompts.why_important: This was the most important decision — I needed a paper I could genuinely own and defend across all parts of the exam, not just submit blindly.

top_5_prompts.prompt: Can you confirm that 'Exact Soft Confidence-Weighted Learning' is published at ICML 2012 and qualifies as a methodological SVM paper?
top_5_prompts.why_important: Ensured the paper satisfied the venue, year, and methodological constraints simultaneously before finalizing my selection.

top_5_prompts.prompt: Does this paper use publicly available datasets? Can it be reproduced on a normal laptop without a GPU?
top_5_prompts.why_important: Reproducibility and compute feasibility are my own responsibility — I needed to confirm this before committing to the paper.

top_5_prompts.prompt: Can you give me the top 5 research papers from ICML, NeurIPS, AISTATS, or KDD between 2010 and 2012 that introduce a new classical method in SVM, GMM, or Time Series?
top_5_prompts.why_important: Gave me a structured set of options to compare rather than searching blindly, saving time and helping me make a more informed final choice.

top_5_prompts.prompt: What exactly qualifies as a methodological contribution under the AML Part A constraints?
top_5_prompts.why_important: Understanding this distinction upfront prevented me from accidentally selecting an application or benchmark paper that would violate the guidelines.

student_declaration.statement: I declare that this JSON file contains a complete and honest record of my LLM usage for Part A. All LLM outputs were used partially and verified independently against the actual paper and exam guidelines. I understand the paper I have selected, and I take full responsibility for its reproducibility, dataset access, and explanation in subsequent exam parts.
